Title: The leucine-responsive regulatory protein SCAB_Lrp modulates thaxtomin biosynthesis, pathogenicity, and morphological development in Streptomyces scabies

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: principal virulence determinant enabling plant pathogenicity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lrp family regulator; N-terminal helix-turn-helix DNA-binding motif and C-terminal effector-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates txtR transcription, thereby increasing txt cluster expression; directly activates amfC, whiB, and ssgB; directly binds its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes thaxtomin A production, virulence/pathogenicity, bacterial growth, and sporulation; deletion reduces thaxtomin A to ~48% of wild type and causes low-virulence phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major direct activator of thaxtomin biosynthesis upstream of the pathway-specific activator txtR
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: autoregulatory binding to its own promoter via the OP site (5'-GGACAGTCGCCGTGCTACG-3')
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: DNA-binding protein-DNA complexes with target promoters; methionine and phenylalanine strengthen binding affinity and complex status
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: transcript detected during thaxtomin production in OBB medium at 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript detected during thaxtomin production in OBB medium at 24 h and 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: oat bran broth for thaxtomin production; soy flour mannitol agar for sporulation phenotype; addition of 10 mM phenylalanine or methionine enhances DNA binding/reporter output
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Paac(3)IV constitutive promoter used to express SCAB_Lrp in E. coli GFP reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: deletion causes reduced mycelial biomass and delayed spore formation; broader developmental effects indicate pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion mutant; complementation; HPLC metabolite quantification; RT-qPCR; EMSA; GFP reporter assays in E. coli; plant virulence bioassays on radish, Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0, and potato tuber slices
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCAB_Lrp is an Lrp-family regulator required for full thaxtomin A production in S. scabies. 2. Deletion of SCAB_Lrp reduced thaxtomin A production to about 48% of wild type and lowered virulence on radish, Arabidopsis, and potato tissues. 3. SCAB_Lrp directly binds the txtR promoter, but not txtA or txtE promoters, and activates transcription of txtR and the thaxtomin biosynthetic cluster. 4. SCAB_Lrp directly activates sporulation-related genes amfC, whiB, and ssgB, linking metabolism with morphological development. 5. SCAB_Lrp autoregulates by binding a specific site in its own promoter. 6. Phenylalanine and methionine act as effectors that strengthen SCAB_Lrp-DNA binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-virulence target for controlling common scab by disrupting SCAB_Lrp activity or its effector-mediated activation; manipulation of SCAB_Lrp or its binding to txtR could suppress thaxtomin production, though pleiotropic effects on growth and sporulation should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper distinguishes SCAB_Lrp as a newly characterized regulator beyond background regulators TxtR and CebR. txtR, amfC, whiB, and ssgB were mechanistic targets but not the main experimental focal genes for structured extraction. No subcellular localization or global omics accession was reported.
